my_thoughts: The ground_reality_check mechanism works: tests definition validity (did anybody come and say this?), questions experiential source (where did this answer come from?). The student's reciting doctrinal answer (conceptual jargon, intellectual recall) shows they understand: witness = conceptual answer (from doctrine, not experience). They'll likely recognize the answer is conceptual, not experiential. Hypothesis: ground reality checks on doctrinal answers expose their conceptual nature—testable by tracking whether students recognize the difference between conceptual and experiential knowledge.
accumulated_insight: Five-turn pattern: T1: Student says 'The inner teacher is not easily reached'—feeling blocked by difficulty via resistance, intellectual barrier. The constraint_collapse mechanism minimizes difficulty, gives instruction (look within), asserts immediacy. T2: Student says 'When I look within, I find sensations and perceptions... I am immersed in this cloud'—transitions to lost in mental content. The ontological_shift mechanism redefines teacher functionally (that which sees), establishes hierarchy (self vs. content). T3: Student says 'I do believe you, but when it comes to the actual finding of this inner self, I find it escapes me'—transitions to vulnerable helplessness. The source_tracing mechanism questions location ('where does it arise?'). T4: Student says 'In the mind'—transitions to mechanically locating experience. The subject_object_inversion mechanism escalates inquiry ('And who knows the mind?'). T5: Student says 'The witness of the mind knows the mind'—reciting doctrinal answer via conceptual jargon, intellectual recall. The ground_reality_check mechanism tests definition validity: did anybody come and say this? Questions experiential source. Pattern: difficulty → look within → find content → escape → locate in mind → doctrinal answer → [next: test validity]. They're grasping: witness = conceptual answer (needs testing).
visible_response: Did anybody come to you and say: 'I am the witness of your mind'?
